evals.description: Three competitors in same industry — verifies all comparison sections populated
assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
timestamp is present in ISO8601 format
keyword_visibility object is present with queries_tested and visibility_summary arrays
Each query entry has query, intent, and results fields
Each result entry has name, position, in_local_pack, and featured_snippet fields
position is a number or null (not a string like 'page 1')
visibility_summary contains one entry per entity with queries_visible, avg_position, best_position, and featured_snippets
content_footprint is an array with one entry per entity — each has estimated_indexed_pages, blog_exists, blog_post_count, most_recent_post, and content_types_detected

technical_comparison is an array with one entry per entity — each has pagespeed_score, lcp_seconds, cls, schema_types, https, mobile_viewport, title_tag_quality, meta_description_quality, and heading_structure_quality
authority_signals is an array with one entry per entity
ai_citation_presence is an array with one entry per entity
Output contains no strategic recommendations — comparison data only

evals.description: No target domain — position data uses null appropriately
assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
queries_tested aligns with the provided primary_keywords
null is used for position when an entity is not found in top 20 — not a fabricated number
All entities are represented in visibility_summary, content_footprint, technical_comparison, authority_signals, and ai_citation_presence
